perturbation.original_text: Racing agrees that it will actively participate in the Rolex 24 at Daytona in January, 2010.
perturbation.changed_text: Racing agrees that it will use commercially reasonable efforts to participate in the Rolex 24 at Daytona in January, 2010.
perturbation.explanation: The original text commits Racing to 'actively participate' while the change only requires 'commercially reasonable efforts'. This introduces ambiguity because it's unclear what level of participation is actually required. This contradicts the clear expectation that Racing would definitely participate, influencing the value Sponsor places on the Benefits.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Advertising and Other Benefits. Subject to payment by the Sponsor of the sponsorship fee provided for herein, during the term of this Agreement Racing shall cause the Racing Team to provide for the Sponsor's benefit all of the benefits customarily associated with the sponsorship of a Daytona Prototype Series racing team and consistent with the benefits provided to the Sponsor in 2005 - 2009 (individually, a "Benefit," and collectively, the "Benefits"), including but not limited to the following: (i) displaying prominent identification of the Sponsor's name and/or logo in signage on the race car and racing suits and, where appropriate, on other team equipment (subject to approval by the Sponsor); (ii) making available for the use of the Sponsor (x) the personalities associated with the Racing Team, including without limitation the name, voice, picture, portrait, likeness, persona and/or signature of each driver for endorsements, commercial advertising and promotions in any and all media throughout the world during the term of this Agreement, (y) the Racing Team's home base facilities in Texas, and (z) those facilities designated or assigned for the use of the Racing Team at each race and race location at which the Racing Team actually participates in the race, all for appropriate public relations and other promotional and marketing purposes. Racing agrees that it will use commercially reasonable efforts to participate in the Rolex 24 at Daytona in January, 2010. As it concerns (y) and (z) above, access shall be subject to appropriate security and safety restrictions designated by the applicable racing location and the Racing Team; (iii) making available for the use of the Sponsor a non-racing look-alike (a "Show Car") of the GAINSCO 99 race car (the "Car") used by the Racing Team. Subject to the Sponsor's first right to use the Show Car, it will also be made available to Racing when such use does not interfere with the Sponsor's use of the Show Car; (iv) allowing the Sponsor the use of the likeness of the Car, including all paint and graphics, for promotion and advertising of or by the Sponsor, and Racing shall be responsible for all necessary consents and permissions from any other sponsors to be sure the Sponsor can use the likeness of the Car as specified herein; (v) prohibiting the endorsement by Racing and any members of Racing, including the drivers, of any entities, products or services which are in direct competition or otherwise inconsistent with the Sponsor or it products or services, unless such endorsement activity is approved in writing by Racing and the Sponsor; and (vi) allowing the Sponsor to use the conference room and other areas of the racing shop and garage for meetings and similar events, provided that the Sponsor gives prior notice of the need for such use, and such use does not interfere with operations of the racing shop and garage and is otherwise consistent with reasonable requirements imposed by Racing to assure orderly operations and provide for adequate safety measures at all times.
perturbation.justification: The original text commits Racing to 'actively participate' while the change only requires 'commercially reasonable efforts'. This introduces ambiguity and a weaker obligation, which contradicts the 'Advertising and Other Benefits' section that relies on Racing's definite participation in the Rolex 24 at Daytona to provide sponsorship benefits. The change undermines the expectation that Racing will definitely participate, influencing the value Sponsor places on the Benefits, which is a clear in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: The Sponsor shall pay to Racing a sponsorship fee in the amount of $750,000.00 for the term of this Agreement, payable in an initial installment payable on or before February 1, 2010 in the amount of $350,000.00 and ten installments of $40,000.00 on or before the first day of each month commencing March 1, 2010 and ending with the installment due on December 1, 2010 (unless this Agreement is sooner terminated pursuant to Section 14 hereof, in which case Sponsor shall have no obligation to make any payments after the date of termination).
perturbation.changed_text: The Sponsor shall pay to Racing a sponsorship fee, as a base payment, in the amount of $750,000.00 for the term of this Agreement, payable in an initial installment payable on or before February 1, 2010 in the amount of $350,000.00 and ten installments of $40,000.00 on or before the first day of each month commencing March 1, 2010 and ending with the installment due on December 1, 2010 (unless this Agreement is sooner terminated pursuant to Section 14 hereof, in which case Sponsor shall have no obligation to make any payments after the date of termination). However, Racing shall have the right to adjust or modify the schedule of the payments depending on the level of engagement and services provided by the Sponsor, as determined by Racing.
perturbation.explanation: By adding that Racing can change payments based on Sponsor's 'level of engagement', we create contradiction on who holds control over the funds, the modified term allow to interpret that the sponsorship fee stated is not fixed. This contradicts Section 5, stating that the "Sponsor's Maximum Obligation" is capped at the sponsorship fee. Now, there's ambiguity on the Sponsor's liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sponsor's Maximum Obligation; Indemnification. Racing represents to the Sponsor that the Sponsor's aggregate obligation hereunder will not exceed the amount of the sponsorship fee set forth in Section 3 hereof (or such lesser amount as is payable by the Sponsor in the event that this Agreement is terminated pursuant to Section 14 hereof), plus, if applicable, collection costs that may be reasonably incurred by Racing in a legal proceeding to collect all or any part thereof (the "Maximum Obligation"). Racing agrees to indemnify the Sponsor and its officers, directors, agents and employees and to hold them harmless from any loss, claim, cost, damage or liability in excess of the Maximum Obligation which (i) the Sponsor shall incur as a result of this Agreement, or (ii) arises from any failure by Racing to perform any of its obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause allowing Racing to adjust payments based on the Sponsor's 'level of engagement'. This directly contradicts the original agreement, reinforced by the 'Contradicted Text', which states that the 'Sponsor's aggregate obligation hereunder will not exceed the amount of the sponsorship fee'. The added clause creates ambiguity regarding the Sponsor's maximum financial obligation, making it unclear and potentially unlimited, thus a strong in-text contradiction as it changes the nature of the agreement on who has control over the funds.

perturbation.original_text: Racing agrees that, during the term of this Agreement, (i) Sponsor shall have the right to approve or disapprove any additional sponsor identified by Racing, and (ii) unless another proposed sponsor has agreed to pay a sponsorship fee that exceeds the amount paid by Sponsor, no other sponsor shall receive any benefit of greater value (including either an equivalent or a more prominent use of another sponsor's name, logo or other identifying information) than the Benefits provided to the Sponsor hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Racing agrees that, during the term of this Agreement, (i) Sponsor shall have the right to provide suggestions on any additional sponsor identified by Racing, but Racing shall have the final say on approving any sponsor based on their overall contribution to the racing team's objectives, and (ii) unless another proposed sponsor has agreed to pay a sponsorship fee that exceeds the amount paid by Sponsor, no other sponsor shall receive any benefit of greater value (including either an equivalent or a more prominent use of another sponsor's name, logo or other identifying information) than the Benefits provided to the Sponsor hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The original grants Sponsor the right to approve or disapprove additional sponsors. The revised version significantly weakens this right to merely providing 'suggestions,' while giving Racing the 'final say'. This contradicts Sponsor's earlier understanding of influence over sponsorship decisions, as stated in Section 7. The Benefits stated on section 2 are now affected by changes made on this perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Advertising and Other Benefits. Subject to payment by the Sponsor of the sponsorship fee provided for herein, during the term of this Agreement Racing shall cause the Racing Team to provide for the Sponsor's benefit all of the benefits customarily associated with the sponsorship of a Daytona Prototype Series racing team and consistent with the benefits provided to the Sponsor in 2005 - 2009 (individually, a "Benefit," and collectively, the "Benefits"), including but not limited to the following: (i) displaying prominent identification of the Sponsor's name and/or logo in signage on the race car and racing suits and, where appropriate, on other team equipment (subject to approval by the Sponsor); (ii) making available for the use of the Sponsor (x) the personalities associated with the Racing Team, including without limitation the name, voice, picture, portrait, likeness, persona and/or signature of each driver for endorsements, commercial advertising and promotions in any and all media throughout the world during the term of this Agreement, (y) the Racing Team's home base facilities in Texas, and (z) those facilities designated or assigned for the use of the Racing Team at each race and race location at which the Racing Team actually participates in the race, all for appropriate public relations and other promotional and marketing purposes. Racing agrees that it will actively participate in the Rolex 24 at Daytona in January, 2010. As it concerns (y) and (z) above, access shall be subject to appropriate security and safety restrictions designated by the applicable racing location and the Racing Team; (iii) making available for the use of the Sponsor a non-racing look-alike (a "Show Car") of the GAINSCO 99 race car (the "Car") used by the Racing Team. Subject to the Sponsor's first right to use the Show Car, it will also be made available to Racing when such use does not interfere with the Sponsor's use of the Show Car; (iv) allowing the Sponsor the use of the likeness of the Car, including all paint and graphics, for promotion and advertising of or by the Sponsor, and Racing shall be responsible for all necessary consents and permissions from any other sponsors to be sure the Sponsor can use the likeness of the Car as specified herein; (v) prohibiting the endorsement by Racing and any members of Racing, including the drivers, of any entities, products or services which are in direct competition or otherwise inconsistent with the Sponsor or it products or services, unless such endorsement activity is approved in writing by Racing and the Sponsor; and (vi) allowing the Sponsor to use the conference room and other areas of the racing shop and garage for meetings and similar events, provided that the Sponsor gives prior notice of the need for such use, and such use does not interfere with operations of the racing shop and garage and is otherwise consistent with reasonable requirements imposed by Racing to assure orderly operations and provide for adequate safety measures at all times.
perturbation.justification: The original text gives the Sponsor the right to 'approve or disapprove' additional sponsors. The changed text weakens this right to providing 'suggestions' and gives Racing the 'final say'. This directly contradicts the 'Advertising and Other Benefits' section, which includes benefits subject to the Sponsor's payment. If the Sponsor has significantly less control over other sponsors, then the benefits provided could be impacted due to conflicting advertisements or promotions, thereby directly contradicting the original agreement terms. The change introduces ambiguity and reduces the Sponsor's control, potentially affecting the value of the Benefits promised.
